title: Two-point Parseval identity for the real DFT

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For the real two-point DFT, spectral energy divided by two equals physical energy.
assumptions: u0 and u1 are real numbers
spectral energy is DC squared plus Nyquist squared
physical energy is u0 squared plus u1 squared
informal.expected_conclusion: The normalized spectral energy equals the physical energy.

declarations.name: dft2Energy
declarations.statement: def dft2Energy (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := dft2DC u0 u1 ^ 2 + dft2Nyquist u0 u1 ^ 2

declarations.name: physical2Energy
declarations.statement: def physical2Energy (u0 u1 : Real) : Real := u0 ^ 2 + u1 ^ 2

declarations.name: dft2_parseval
declarations.statement: theorem dft2_parseval (u0 u1 : Real) : dft2Energy u0 u1 / 2 = physical2Energy u0 u1

tasks.input: Translate the two-point Parseval identity into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem dft2_parseval (u0 u1 : Real) : dft2Energy u0 u1 / 2 = physical2Energy u0 u1

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by rewriting with the doubled-energy identity and simplifying.
tasks.target: Rewrite by dft2Energy_eq_two_physical2Energy and use ring.

tasks.input: Find the LeanNumerics lemma needed to prove the normalized two-point Parseval identity.
